text.title: API-First Principles and Toolchain
text.summary: Core principles of API-first development, the design-to-deploy workflow, and the canonical toolchain for OpenAPI 3.1.

rule.statement: Design the API contract (OpenAPI spec) before writing any implementation code.
rule.rationale: The contract is the first artifact. All downstream work — server stubs, client SDKs, mock servers, documentation, contract tests — is generated from or validated against the spec. This prevents implementation-driven API shapes that are inconsistent across services.

rule.statement: Use the spec as the single source of truth: generate code, tests, and docs from it, never the reverse.
rule.rationale: When code is the source of truth, documentation drifts within weeks. Generating from spec ensures structural consistency; deviations are caught by Spectral linting and contract tests (Dredd, Pact).

rule.statement: Design endpoints for API consumers, not for implementation convenience.
rule.rationale: Consumer-driven design surfaces usability problems before code is written. Implementation-convenient designs often expose internal data structures, forcing consumers to do transformation work the API should handle.

rule.statement: Deploy a mock server (Prism) from the spec before any backend code exists.
rule.rationale: Mock servers enable parallel frontend/backend development. Frontend teams build against the contract; backend teams implement against it. Both converge on the same interface without blocking each other.

section.title: Workflow
section.p: DESIGN → VALIDATE → MOCK → IMPLEMENT → TEST → DEPLOY

list.item: DESIGN: Write OpenAPI 3.1 YAML spec (endpoints, schemas, auth, errors)
VALIDATE: Run Spectral linting; fix errors before proceeding
MOCK: Start Prism mock server; share with frontend team
IMPLEMENT: Generate server stubs from spec; implement business logic
TEST: Run Dredd or Pact contract tests against running server
DEPLOY: Gate on contract tests passing in CI before merge

rule.statement: Use OpenAPI 3.1 (not 3.0) for all new specs; use type: ['string', 'null'] for nullable fields, not nullable: true.
rule.rationale: OAS 3.1 aligns with full JSON Schema 2020-12 vocabulary. The nullable: true pattern is OAS 3.0-only and produces incorrect schema validation in 3.1-aware tools. Agents default to 3.0 patterns — specify version explicitly in every prompt.

list.item: JSON Schema alignment: full JSON Schema vocabularies supported (not subset)
Webhooks: new top-level element for event-driven/outbound APIs
$ref improvements: better reference handling, circular refs supported
Nullable: use type: ['string', 'null'] not nullable: true
Linting: Spectral (configurable rules), Redocly CLI (bundle + lint)
Mocking: Prism (mock server from spec), WireMock (stateful mocking)
Code generation: openapi-generator-cli (server stubs + client SDKs)
Contract testing: Dredd (spec vs. server), Pact (consumer-driven)
Breaking change detection: bump diff (CI diff on every PR)

text.reference: Lint spec with Spectral + start Prism mock server
